title: Paspalum glabrinode
taxonomy: Paspalum glabrinode is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Paspalum, which consists of a wide range of grasses native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further classified within the subfamily Panicoideae, a large and diverse group of grasses. Paspalum glabrinode is a type of perennial grass that is commonly found in warm and humid climates. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses and its unique characteristics as a member of the Paspalum genus.

intro: Paspalum glabrinode, also known as 'Cortadura' or 'False Couch Grass', is a plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum glabrinode include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
